chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Criminal Revision Application No.S-25 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 3. For hearing of M.A. No.2181/2022. 28.03.2022 Miyan Taj Muhammad Keerio, Advocate for the applicant. Mr. Muhammad Ali Noonari, Assistant Prosecutor General, Sindh. == The applicant on conclusion of trial was convicted under section 380, 457 and 427 P.P.C and sentenced to various terms of imprisonment with fine with benefit of section 382-B Cr.P.C by learned 2nd Judicial Magistrate Johi vide Judgment dated 09.12.2021, it was impugned by the applicant by preferring an appeal, it was dismissed by learned 1st Additional Sessions Judge/MCTC, Dadu, vide judgment dated 19.02.2022, which is impugned by the applicant before this Court by preferring the instant criminal revision application, and in the meanwhile, he has sought for suspension of the sentence awarded to him and his release on bail. It is contended by learned counsel for the applicant aggregate sentence awarded to the applicant is spreading over three years with fine which is short one; the applicant has already remained in jail for 8 months and hearing of a revision application of the applicant is likely to take time. By contending so, he sought for suspension of the sentence and release of the applicant on bail. Learned Assistant Prosecutor General for the State has recorded no objection to release of the applicant on bail pending disposal of his revision application. Heard arguments and perused the record. Obviously the aggregate conviction/sentence awarded to the applicant being three years with fine is short one; he has already remained in jail for about eight months and hearing of his revision application is likely to take time, therefore, while relying upon case of Abdul Hameed Vs.

Heard arguments and perused the record. Obviously the aggregate conviction/sentence awarded to the applicant being three years with fine is short one; he has already remained in jail for about eight months and hearing of his revision application is likely to take time, therefore, while relying upon case of Abdul Hameed Vs. Muhammad Abdullah and others (1999 SCMR- 2589), the sentence awarded to the applicant is suspended, consequently, he is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in sum of Rs.50,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. Listed application (M.A.No.2184/2022) is disposed of accordingly. Adjourned to date in office for hearing of revision application. JUDGE Muhammad Danish*
